panels.description: Wide shot inside a moonlit cave mouth in the Seeonee hills. Father Wolf, a large, powerful gray wolf, stretches and yawns, looking out with a calm expression. Mother Wolf lies beside him, her big gray nose resting on four small, tumbling, squealing wolf cubs. Soft, natural moonlight illuminates the serene scene, casting gentle shadows.

bubbles.text: Augrh! It is time to hunt again.

panels.description: Medium shot from inside the cave looking out. Tabaqui, a gaunt, yellow-eyed jackal with shifty eyes and ragged fur, slinks cautiously into view at the moonlit cave entrance. Father Wolf is visible in the foreground, partially obscured in shadow, watching Tabaqui with a wary, disapproving gaze. Moonlit jungle vegetation is visible in the background.

bubbles.text: It was the jackal—Tabaqui, the Dish-licker—and the wolves of India despise Tabaqui.
But they are afraid of him too, because Tabaqui, more than anyone else in the jungle, is apt to go mad...

panels.description: Medium shot: Father Wolf, with a stiff, stern expression and rigid posture, addresses Tabaqui. Tabaqui, still partly outside the cave entrance, shows a hint of a sneer on his face, his eyes darting quickly. The cave interior is dimly lit, contrasting with the moonlight outside.

bubbles.text: Enter, then, and look, but there is no food here.

panels.description: Medium-close shot: Tabaqui, now deeper inside the cave, greedily cracking the end of a buck's bone with some meat on it. His yellow eyes dart slyly towards the unseen wolves, an unsettling smirk on his snout. The cave interior is dark, with only a little light reaching him, emphasizing his shadowy presence.

bubbles.text: For a wolf, no, but for so mean a person as myself a dry bone is a good feast. Who are we, the Gidur-log, to pick and choose?

panels.description: Medium close-up: Tabaqui's face, now turned towards the unseen cubs, wears a wide, unsettling grin, his eyes sparkling with a malicious glint. In the slightly blurred background, Mother Wolf's fur is subtly bristling, indicating a protective posture. Soft moonlight catches Tabaqui's unsettling smile, highlighting his sinister intent.

bubbles.text: All thanks for this good meal. How beautiful are the noble children! How large are their eyes! And so young too!
Now, Tabaqui knew as well as anyone else that there is nothing so unlucky as to compliment children to their faces.
It pleased him to see Mother and Father Wolf look uncomfortable.

panels.description: Medium close-up: Tabaqui's face, now devoid of its previous grin, appears more serious and menacing as he delivers crucial news. Shadows deepen around him, emphasizing his ominous message. In the blurred background, the shocked and uneasy expressions of Father and Mother Wolf are faintly visible.

bubbles.text: Shere Khan, the Big One, has shifted his hunting grounds. He will hunt among these hills for the next moon, so he has told me.

panels.description: Close-up: Father Wolf's face, snarling furiously, teeth bared in a defiant growl. His eyes are narrowed with intense anger, and his fur bristles. Dynamic, heavy shadows exaggerate his expression, highlighting his outrage. The background is a dark, blurred cave wall, emphasizing his singular fury.

bubbles.text: Shere Khan was the tiger who lived near the Waingunga River, twenty miles away.
He has no right! By the Law of the Jungle he has no right to change his quarters without due warning. He will frighten every head of game within ten miles, and I--I have to kill for two, these days.
